name: Italy
tagline: A country best read slowly, with a glass in hand.
blurb: Sun-drenched coastlines give way to hilltop towns where time moves at the speed of the grape harvest. Italy rewards those who slow down — a perfect espresso, a slow-cooked ragù, the particular quiet of a church at noon. Every region is its own country, and every region is worth visiting.
dish: Cacio e Pepe with a Negroni
playlist: Lucio Battisti — Con il nastro rosa

name: Japan
tagline: Precision, stillness, neon. A country of quiet gestures.
blurb: A country of extraordinary precision where ancient ritual and modern spectacle coexist without apology. Visit in spring for cherry blossoms, in autumn for the fiery maple canopy — both seasons stop time in their own way. Everywhere is layered: temples behind convenience stores, forests beside bullet train lines.
playlist: Ryuichi Sakamoto — Merry Christmas Mr. Lawrence

name: Morocco
tagline: Saffron, silver, and the call to prayer across rooftops.
blurb: A country that assaults the senses in the finest way — saffron spice stalls, labyrinthine medinas, and the vast silence of the Sahara just hours away. Morocco is the antidote to sameness. The Atlas Mountains divide the coastal cosmopolitan from the ancient erg, and both are worth your time.

name: Iceland
tagline: Fire under ice. A landscape that refuses to settle.
blurb: Where volcanic activity sculpts the landscape daily and the aurora borealis writes its own headline. Iceland is a place of elemental extremes — hot springs and glaciers, darkness and midnight sun, all within a day's drive. The Ring Road is not a route; it is an education.

name: Peru
tagline: Walking through cloud forests into civilizations older than memory.
blurb: Ancient Incan architecture clings to cloud-wrapped Andean peaks while Lima's coastal restaurants redefine what South American cuisine can be. Peru is a country of staggering altitude and even more staggering ambition. The Inca Trail earns every step.
playlist: Chabuca Granda — La flor de la canela

name: France
tagline: Bread warm from the oven, a carafe of rosé, an opinion.
blurb: No country has elevated the everyday into an art form with such unapologetic confidence — a butcher's window, a morning café, a Sunday market. France is less a destination than a continuous lesson in living well. The regions are each worth a trip: the Loire, Burgundy, the Basque coast.
dish: Steak frites with a Côtes du Rhône
playlist: Françoise Hardy — Le temps de l'amour

name: Australia
tagline: A continent that measures distance in horizons.
blurb: From the electric blue of the Great Barrier Reef to the ochre silence of Uluru, Australia is built at a scale that recalibrates the imagination. The cities are excellent; the wilderness is what you'll remember. Drive the Great Ocean Road and understand why Australians are relaxed about everything else.
dish: Fresh oysters with Clare Valley Riesling

name: India
tagline: A country that arrives as a feeling before a place.
blurb: To arrive in India is to be immediately, overwhelmingly reminded that the world is larger than any itinerary can contain. Temples and bazaars and backwater sunsets — each one a sensory complete world of its own. The food alone could justify the journey ten times over.
playlist: Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan — Tumhein Dillagi

name: Egypt
tagline: Stone, river, myth. In that order, for 5,000 years.
blurb: The sheer fact of the pyramids — that they exist, that they're as magnificent as advertised — does something irreversible to the imagination. Egypt pairs its ancient grandeur with the living, breathing theatre of the Nile. Luxor at night, lit up against the desert sky, is unforgettable.

name: Mexico
tagline: Marigolds, mezcal, and a beat you feel before you hear.
blurb: Mexico is the country that keeps exceeding expectations — baroque cathedrals, world-class contemporary art, and food so good it has UNESCO status. The variety is staggering: colonial heartlands, Pacific surf towns, pre-Columbian ruins. Oaxaca alone is worth the flight.
dish: Tacos al pastor with salsa verde

name: Thailand
tagline: The smell of basil, jasmine, diesel, rain — all at once.
blurb: Thai hospitality is legendary for a reason — warmth is structural here, not incidental. From the languid islands of the Andaman Sea to the frenetic beauty of Bangkok's street food scene, Thailand delivers on every register. It remains one of the world's great travel bargains.
playlist: Khruangbin — The Universe Smiles Upon You

name: Greece
tagline: Wine-dark seas and whitewashed patience.
blurb: The Aegean light is a phenomenon — there's no adequate preparation for how it makes white plaster walls and blue water look. Greece is most itself outside of high season, when the crowds thin and the kafeneion fills back up. The food is better than you remember from last time.

name: Spain
tagline: Dinner at ten, dancing at one, churros at six.
blurb: Spain has seventeen autonomous communities, each with its own gastronomy, dialect, and fierce sense of identity. The food alone — pintxos, jamón, Rioja — justifies the journey; everything else is bonus. San Sebastián consistently serves some of the most concentrated great cooking on earth.

name: Vietnam
tagline: A soundtrack of motorbike horns and slurping pho.
blurb: A country shaped by geography as much as history — from the misty mountain terraces of Sapa to the limestone karsts of Halong Bay to the ancient trading port of Hoi An. Vietnam is long, beautiful, and impossible to exhaust in a single trip. Come back.
playlist: Hoàng Thùy Linh — Để Mị Nói Cho Mà Nghe

name: Portugal
tagline: Melancholy and sunshine, perfected.
blurb: Small in footprint, enormous in feeling — Portugal has been quietly perfecting its coastal cuisine, tiled facades, and soulful music for centuries without needing anyone's approval. Lisbon is the best walking city in Europe, and the Douro Valley in harvest season is one of the most beautiful places on earth.
dish: Pastel de nata, warm from the oven

name: South Africa
tagline: Two oceans meet, and the sky stays a size bigger here.
blurb: Two oceans, eleven official languages, and a landscape of almost improbable variety — South Africa asks you to expand your definition of what a single country can contain. The wine is superb; the safaris are the real thing. Cape Town may be the world's most beautifully situated city.

name: New Zealand
tagline: Rivers you could drink from. Silences you can feel.
blurb: The landscape here looks designed by a committee of geographers trying to include everything — fjords, geothermal fields, vineyards, surf beaches, and peaks that belong in an old Romantic painting. Somehow it's all real. The South Island alone is one of the great road-trip destinations.

name: Turkey
tagline: Two continents, served with strong coffee and stronger coffee.
blurb: Istanbul alone — straddling two continents, carrying thirty centuries of empire — would justify the journey. But Cappadocia's cave hotels and the turquoise coast's gulet sailing make a compelling case for staying longer. Turkish breakfast is among the world's great culinary rituals.

name: Kenya
tagline: Dust, acacia, and a heartbeat of hooves.
blurb: The annual migration of wildebeest across the Mara River is one of nature's unrepeatable spectacles, but Kenya offers much more: the dhow-sailed Swahili coast, the cedar forests of the Aberdare Range, and Nairobi's extraordinary restaurant scene. The light in the Mara at golden hour is not improvable.
playlist: Hugh Masekela — Grazing in the Grass

name: Brazil
tagline: Samba is a condition here, not a dance.
blurb: The largest country in South America contains multitudes — five time zones, every biome, a city of carnival and Christ, and an Amazon so vast it generates its own weather. Brazilian hospitality is the warmth of a country that genuinely wants you to stay longer.

name: Norway
tagline: Fjords so still they become mirrors of themselves.
blurb: The fjords are not a postcard cliché — they are a physical phenomenon that requires a physical presence to understand. Norway's long winter offers the northern lights; the summer brings the midnight sun and wildflower meadows that make the drive north feel like entering a different season of consciousness.

name: Argentina
tagline: Steak, Malbec, and a tango you didn't plan on.
blurb: Buenos Aires earns its reputation as the Paris of the South, but the real Argentina is the one that stretches south into Patagonia — where glaciers calve into lakes and the wind has opinions. Come for the steak; stay for the silence. Mendoza's wine country fills in what's between.

name: Nepal
tagline: Prayer flags on thin air, higher than you've been.
blurb: The Himalayas are humbling at any altitude, but Nepal earns its place beyond the trekking routes — in the quiet courtyards of Kathmandu's temple complexes, in the medieval streets of Bhaktapur, in the particular light of the Pokhara valley. The people are as extraordinary as the mountains.

name: Cuba
tagline: A country living between rum, music, and pastel memory.
blurb: Havana exists in its own time zone — classic cars, peeling baroque facades, and a music scene that makes every evening feel like the last night before everything changes. Cuba rewards the traveller who arrives without expectations and leaves without conclusions.
playlist: Buena Vista Social Club — Chan Chan

name: Indonesia
tagline: 17,000 islands, each making its own weather.
blurb: Bali is the introduction; the archipelago is the education. From the Hindu ceremonies of Ubud to the Komodo dragons of Flores to the coral reefs of Raja Ampat, Indonesia rewards those who look beyond the brochure. One country, a thousand worlds.

name: Jordan
tagline: Pink stone cities and the salt of a dead sea.
blurb: Petra carved from rose-red sandstone is extraordinary enough, but Jordan's greatest achievement may be the warmth of its people — a hospitality tradition so ingrained it feels constitutional. Wadi Rum at sunset is another planet entirely, and one of the best arguments for sleeping under the stars.

name: United Kingdom
tagline: A kettle is always on, and a story is never finished.
blurb: The British Isles resist summarizing — Scotland's Highlands are an entirely different world from the Cotswolds' stone walls, which are entirely different from London's boundless cultural energy. The weather is character-building. The pub is the civilizational achievement.
playlist: The Smiths — There Is a Light That Never Goes Out

name: Ireland
tagline: Green in forty languages, all of them sung.
blurb: The west coast of Ireland is where green finally runs out of synonyms. Pub sessions that start at eight and end nowhere, cliffs that require you to stand still for a moment, a literary tradition that makes every stranger a potential raconteur. The craic is not a marketing slogan.

name: Scotland
tagline: Wind, whisky, and the kind of weather that has opinions.
blurb: The Highland landscape is one of the most emotionally evocative in the world — glens, lochs, and the ruins of castles that look better in the rain. Edinburgh's stone cityscape and whisky culture complete a picture of extraordinary depth. The Isle of Skye rewards every approach.
playlist: Julie Fowlis — Thig Crioch Air An T-saoghal

name: Switzerland
tagline: Clean cold air. Cheese that takes itself seriously.
blurb: The Alps are the architect and Switzerland is the very careful client. Everything functions, the trains arrive, the fondue is taken seriously, and the view from above the treeline is a permanent rebuke to anyone who has settled for less. Arrive in winter or summer — both are correct.

name: Colombia
tagline: Coffee, color, and a rhythm that refuses to sit down.
blurb: Colombia's transformation is one of the great travel stories of the twenty-first century — and the country is aware of it, proud of it, and ready to show you everything that was always there: the coffee, the cumbia, the colonial cities. Cartagena's walled old town at dusk is magnificent.

name: Chile
tagline: From driest desert to deepest south — a country of edges.
blurb: Chile is the longest country on earth, which means it contains a cross-section of the planet: the driest desert, the deepest fjords, the southernmost city, and some of the world's best wine growing in the Casablanca Valley between. Torres del Paine is worth any approach.

name: South Korea
tagline: A country that updates itself daily without losing its temper.
blurb: Seoul operates at a frequency unlike any other capital — organized chaos, technological ambition, and a reverence for both ancient ceremony and tomorrow's trend. Korean food culture alone — the banchan, the BBQ, the jjigae — is worth the journey. The countryside is entirely different and entirely necessary.

name: United Arab Emirates
tagline: Glass towers on ancient dunes. Dates still taste the same.
blurb: The contrast is the point — ancient trade routes and future skylines, desert silence and the world's largest shopping mall. Abu Dhabi's Sheikh Zayed mosque is genuinely among the most beautiful spaces ever built. The desert overnight is the corrective.

name: Singapore
tagline: A city where three cuisines live on one street corner.
blurb: Singapore is a city of improbable achievement — a hawker centre curry for five dollars, a Michelin-starred meal down the road, and Gardens by the Bay glowing blue at night. It is very small and somehow very big. The food is the most concentrated great eating in Asia.

name: Georgia
tagline: Where every meal is a supra and every toast is for you.
blurb: Georgia is the world's best-kept travel secret, and the Caucasian secret is getting out. Ancient cave monasteries, natural wine from 8,000-year-old traditions, and a capital whose sulfur baths and bohemian energy feel invented for the curious traveller. Tbilisi in autumn is close to perfect.

name: Canada
tagline: An apology-shaped country with landscapes that aren't sorry.
blurb: Canada's scale is the experience — Banff's glacier-fed lakes, the boreal forest's endless quiet, Quebec City's centuries of French sensibility in North America. The country rewards anyone who resists the urge to summarize it. Tofino's surf and Yukon's silence are both necessary.

name: United States
tagline: A highway anthology — each exit a different country.
blurb: The American highway is a philosophical exercise as much as a journey — through the desert Southwest, the Pacific Coast, the bayou South. The cities are conversations; the land between them is the argument. No country rewards the detour more.
dish: Oysters in Maine, BBQ in Texas

name: Philippines
tagline: 7,641 islands, and your ferry leaves in ten minutes.
blurb: Seven thousand islands and the warmth to match — Filipinos have an infectiousness to their hospitality that transforms every trip into something personal. Palawan's limestone karsts rising from clear water is a scene that doesn't need exaggeration. The tricycles and the karaoke are part of the deal.

name: Laos
tagline: A country that runs on the Mekong and morning alms.
blurb: Laos is what the rest of Southeast Asia was twenty years ago — deliberate, unhurried, Buddhist in its architecture and its pace. Luang Prabang's gilded temples and French colonial guesthouses make for an unlikely, entirely compelling harmony. The alms-giving ceremony at dawn is not to be rushed through.

name: Cambodia
tagline: Temples reclaimed by trees, then by tourists, then by trees again.
blurb: Angkor Wat at sunrise is one of the non-negotiable experiences of a life spent travelling — a city reclaimed by the jungle and then opened back up to the world. Cambodia's resilience and warmth put context around everything you see. Stay beyond Siem Reap and you'll understand the country.

name: Tanzania
tagline: Kilimanjaro at dawn; Zanzibar at dusk. Balance achieved.
blurb: From the great plains of the Serengeti to Kilimanjaro's glaciated summit to the spice-scented alleys of Stone Town, Tanzania asks you to constantly recalibrate your sense of scale. The migration is not a wildlife moment; it is a geological event.

name: Ethiopia
tagline: The cradle, served with the freshest coffee on earth.
blurb: Ethiopia is the world's original coffee country, the home of Lucy, and a land of rock-hewn churches that make medieval European cathedrals look recently built. It is ancient, proud, and quietly startling to anyone arriving without expectations. The Lalibela churches at Christmas are extraordinary.

name: Madagascar
tagline: Evolution's private studio. No two trees the same.
blurb: Madagascar is where evolution ran its own experiment for sixty million years, undisturbed — the lemurs and the baobabs and the Tsingy stone forests exist nowhere else on earth. It is alien, beautiful, and entirely worth the complicated journey. The Avenue of the Baobabs at sunset is its own argument.

name: Namibia
tagline: Dunes as tall as mountains. Skies wider than the Atlantic.
blurb: The Namib is the oldest desert on earth, and the dunes of Sossusvlei at first light — red-orange against the blue void — are among the most photographed places in Africa for good reason. Namibia's emptiness is the entire point. The Skeleton Coast is the world's most dramatic coastline.

name: Sweden
tagline: Fika is a verb. And an organizing principle.
blurb: Sweden has made a lifestyle out of balance — work and rest, darkness and summer light, tradition and design innovation. The Stockholm archipelago in summer is a Nordic fantasy; Lapland in winter is a different planet entirely. The design sensibility is not a mood board; it is a way of living.
playlist: First Aid Kit — My Silver Lining

name: Croatia
tagline: The Adriatic with a thousand doors. All unlocked.
blurb: The Adriatic coastline is legitimately one of the most beautiful in Europe — a thousand islands, Roman ruins in city centres, and a wine tradition in Dalmatia that has been making exceptional reds since antiquity. Dubrovnik needs no advertisement; Hvar is the argument for staying.

name: Bolivia
tagline: A salt flat so big the sky walks on it.
blurb: Bolivia operates at an altitude that makes everything slightly more intense — the light, the colours, the way the salt flats of Uyuni reflect the sky as though the ground has become the mirror. It is one of the most photogenic countries on earth and one of the least visited.

name: Germany
tagline: Order, then beer, then order again.
blurb: Germany's range surprises those who arrive expecting only efficiency — the hedonism of Berlin's nightlife, the Baroque excess of the Bavarian palaces, the gentle pace of a Rhine Valley cycle route in autumn. The beer, naturally, is taken with the same seriousness as everything else.

name: Netherlands
tagline: Flat land, tall people, bigger bicycles.
blurb: The Dutch have been making land out of water for five centuries, which tells you something about their character. Amsterdam's canal belt is a UNESCO-listed urban masterpiece; the cycling culture ensures you see it at the right pace. The tulip fields in April are quietly overwhelmingly beautiful.
